title: Fact-checking transparency principles
summary: Reliable fact-checking explains sources, methodology, funding, corrections, and why a claim received a rating.
claims: A verification system should disclose its evidence and method.
Fact checks should be transparent about sources and corrections.

title: ClaimReview structured data
summary: ClaimReview provides a structured way to represent a reviewed claim, its author, the item reviewed, and the review rating.
claims: A fact-check record should preserve the claim, author, review body, and review rating.
Claim-level verification is more auditable than article-level labels alone.

title: Fact Check Tools API
summary: The Fact Check Tools API lets applications search fact-check articles and ClaimReview markup from fact-checking publishers.
claims: External fact-check APIs can supplement local retrieval.
ClaimReview-compatible results help compare a submitted claim with existing fact checks.

title: Verifiable credentials data model
summary: Verifiable credentials describe tamper-evident claims about subjects, issuers, and proofs that can support source identity checks.
claims: Credential verification should identify issuer, subject, proof, and status.
Source credentials are separate from whether a specific claim is true.

title: C2PA content provenance
source: Coalition for Content Provenance and Authenticity
summary: C2PA manifests can attach provenance assertions to media, helping users inspect origin and editing history.
claims: Media provenance signals help evaluate photos and videos attached to news.
Absence of provenance is not proof that media is false.

title: Health claim verification baseline
summary: High-risk health news should be checked against primary institutions, clinical evidence, exact dates, named experts, and conflicts of interest.
claims: A health cure claim, including a cancer cure claim, needs strong clinical evidence before it can be treated as verified.
Claims about unnamed pills curing cancer overnight are high risk without named trials and institutions.
Anonymous viral posts are weak evidence for medical claims.

title: Financial and market claim verification baseline
summary: Market-moving claims require primary filings, company statements, exchange notices, or credible wire confirmation before publication.
claims: A company acquisition rumor is not verified without filings, company statements, or multiple high-credibility reports.
Market news needs timestamps because stale reports can be misleading.

title: Breaking disaster verification baseline
summary: Breaking disaster reporting should separate confirmed official facts from unverified witness accounts and old media recirculation.
claims: Breaking news should preserve uncertainty when only one source is available.
Images and videos need timestamp, location, and provenance checks.
